summary: Build-A-Bear has transitioned from a mall-based children's retailer into a diversified brand-equity business with a large, loyal ADULT fan base. Crucially, that adult demand is online- and community-led: grown fans engage through TikTok, Reddit, owned digital experiences and e-commerce rather than mall visits, and physical stores increasingly skew family and tourist traffic. This tracker monitors independent online demand signals — social, community, search, digital, and the secondary market — to assess whether adult-driven brand demand is durable and broadening.
convergence: Most independent signals — search interest, the owned social footprint, the Reddit community, Roblox engagement, the online audience mix and secondary-market pricing — point the same way: a structural, adult-led brand. The notable exception is e-commerce ORDER VOLUME, which has softened recently — we flag it rather than smooth it. Convergence across unrelated platforms (with the divergence called out) is what distinguishes a durable demand shift from a single-channel fad.
bottom_line: Bottom line: independent online signals — U.S. search interest at a 5-year high (trailing 12-month average ~+90% YoY), a TikTok-led social footprint, a compounding collector community, 24M+ Roblox visits, and four/five-figure resale clears — corroborate a real, adult-led demand base that is increasingly online and decoupled from mall foot traffic, while the P&L diversifies toward higher-margin commercial and franchising on record FY2025 revenue.
caveats: E-commerce ORDER DEMAND has rolled over: negative for three straight quarters and −26.1% in Q1 FY26 on softer consumer traffic. The online audience is structurally adult (25–34, female-skewing), but volume re-acceleration is the key watch-item of the bull case.
Reddit subscriber history is now real (Wayback Machine archives, ~6.6K 2022 → ~72K Jan-2026); only the conversation-themes breakdown remains qualitative rather than a measured post classification.
Resale is a documented but small sample of notable sales, not a continuous price index.
Tariffs (~$11M in FY2025) and a maturing U.S. mall base are real margin/footprint headwinds.
